description: Privileged access to Firefox internals for SmartWindow model context inspection.

functions.name: getSmartWindowData
functions.description: Collects SmartWindow model context from browser internals.

functions.name: getMemories
functions.description: Returns stored SmartWindow memories ({id, memory_summary}) — used by the popup to let the user select memories for groundtruth.tagged_memories.

functions.name: getTools
functions.description: Returns the registered SmartWindow tools normalized to [{name, description, args: [{name, type, required, description, enum?}]}] — used by the popup to render per-tool argument inputs for groundtruth.tools.

functions.name: getUserMessageCount
functions.description: Returns the number of real user messages in the active SmartWindow conversation, skipping mocked-system messages (real-time context, relevant memories). Used to drive turn-conditional argument requirements (e.g. run_search.query is required on turns 2+).

functions.name: getCompactedConversation
functions.description: Returns the compacted SmartWindow conversation messages (post-PromptOptimizer), with auto-injected system/user messages (system prompt, real-time context, relevant memories) filtered out. Used by the basic export view to pre-fill questions based on the conversation.

functions.name: getRawConversation
functions.description: Returns the raw ChatConversation messages, untouched by the openAI-format conversion. Roles are integers (0=user, 1=assistant) and assistant messages carry metadata like `memoriesApplied`. Used by the basic export view's tagged-memories pre-fill.

functions.name: getOpenTabs
functions.description: Returns the currently open tabs in the most recent browser window — [{url, title, isActiveTab}]. Used by the basic export view to let users pick which tabs they expected the assistant to reference.

functions.name: exportToFile
functions.description: Opens a native Save dialog and writes collected data to the chosen file.

functions.name: basicExportToFile
functions.description: Basic (non-expert) export — opens the Save dialog and writes a slimmed-down SmartWindow data payload. Template entry point; the exact options shape will evolve as the basic view's fields are designed.

functions.name: triggerExport
functions.description: Shows the export dialog in the active SmartWindow page and exports to file. Used by the keyboard shortcut so it works even when the toolbar is hidden.

functions.name: init
functions.description: Injects the export button into all open browser windows and watches for new ones.

functions.name: uninit
functions.description: Removes the injected export button from all windows.
